AI模型发布.title: OpenAI - Wikipedia
AI模型发布.content: # OpenAI. In July, the United States Department of Defense announced that OpenAI had received a $200 million contract for AI in the military, along with Anthropic, Google, and xAI "XAI (company)"). In December 2025, it was announced OpenAI had agreed to acquire Neptune, an AI tooling startup that helps companies track and manage model training, for an undisclosed amount. In December 2022, OpenAI received widespread media coverage after launching a free preview of ChatGPT, its new AI chatbot based on GPT-3.5. On November 20, 2023, Microsoft CEO Satya Nadella announced Altman and Brockman would be joining Microsoft to lead a new advanced AI research team, but added that they were still committed to OpenAI despite recent events. On November 29, 2023, OpenAI announced that an anonymous Microsoft employee had joined the board as a non-voting member to observe the company's operations; Microsoft resigned from the board in July 2024.

创业公司.title: AI for Startups | Andreessen Horowitz
创业公司.content: a16z published its policy agenda for Little Tech in July, proposing a series of policy ideas to promote AI innovation through regulation that creates an even playing field across all industries. Microsoft is committed to making large-scale AI infrastructure investments that only a Big Tech company with our scope and size can afford, creating a platform that is affordable and easily accessible to everyone, including startups and small firms. Microsoft and a16z jointly recognize an opportunity to advance public policy that will allow American entrepreneurs and innovators to do what they do best—build new tools and businesses that solve problems, create new jobs and opportunities, and enable startups to flourish. * **Invest in AI:** the U.S. government should invest in AI to accelerate American innovation, strengthen our national security, and create economic opportunity. * **Help people thrive in an AI-enabled world:** building a new AI economy that supports startups, and American entrepreneurship will require public policy that cultivates technical talent and engages digital citizens.

创业公司.title: How to Build an AI Startup: Go Big, Be Strange, Embrace ... - WIRED
创业公司.content: The goal was not to try to pick winners but to see what it’s like, on the ground, to build AI products—how AI tools have changed the nature of their work; how terrifying it is to compete in a crowded field. Lee told me how a year ago they spent three months learning and suffering to meticulously code up an agent, and that now all their work “could probably be written in three days, without our touch.” Lee confessed to feeling a bit bleak and finds himself wondering what’s even worth learning these days. When, as some like to say, you can write a startup’s worth of code in a weekend, the work of building a tech company is no longer coding, exactly. I’d reached out to a company called Starcloud that’s working to put data centers in space (to help run all that AI, naturally). OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and a host of other major tech companies have found common ground in F/ai, a new startup accelerator based out of Paris.

AI应用.title: What Is an AI Application?
AI应用.content: # What Is an AI Application? AI applications are designed to automate and enhance processes, improve efficiency, and provide insights that would be difficult or impossible for humans to achieve on their own. ## Benefits of AI Applications. Overall, the integration of AI applications into different industries leads to more efficient operations, better decision-making, enhanced customer experiences, and improved safety and quality. ## Applications of AI in Various Industries. AI applications in the automotive industry are driving innovations and improving safety and efficiency. AI applications in manufacturing streamline processes, improve quality, and reduce costs. AI applications benefit businesses by automating repetitive tasks, enhancing data analysis for better decision-making, improving customer service with chatbots, and optimizing operations such as supply chain management and inventory control. Deploying an AI application involves several challenges, including data quality and availability, the need for significant computational resources, ensuring algorithm transparency and fairness, addressing security and privacy concerns, and integrating AI solutions into existing systems and workflows.

AI应用.title: What Are AI Applications? Real-World Use Cases & ...
AI应用.content: ## What Are AI Applications? AI applications are software programs that use artificial intelligence techniques such as machine learning, natural language processing (NLP) and robotics to perform various tasks. Organizations across numerous industries, from healthcare to retail and beyond, are using AI applications to improve efficiency, reduce human errors, glean more insight from their data, reduce costs and enhance decision-making. ## Top Applications of AI. #### What is the most used AI application? Learn how it works, explore types of generative AI, and see how it differs from traditional AI with real-world examples.#### Artificial Intelligence in Business: Impact & Examples. Learn how to use AI in advertising with real-world examples of automation and predictive insights.#### Data Visualization: Techniques & Real-World Examples. Discover the benefits of data visualization, top techniques, and real-world examples to improve data-driven decision-making and communication.#### AI in Business Intelligence: Benefits and Use Cases.

研究论文.title: AI Research - Artificial Intelligence at Northwestern
研究论文.content: # ARTIFICIAL INTELLIGENCE AT NORTHWESTERN. ## AI Research. Artificial intelligence (AI) research explores the nature of intelligence and the ways in which computation can be used to explain and engineer it. Work in AI combines the scale afforded by machine learning with the expressive and organizational power of semantic information-processing and knowledge-based reasoning. Our faculty research programs include work in:. * robotics and human-robot interaction. Meet faculty who work in this area. ### Featured Research. Training Machines without Massive Data Research Image. #### Training Machines to Answer Questions without Massive Data. Ken Forbus’s Qualitative Reasoning Group is working toward training machines to answer questions without massive data. His group has developed a more human method to train AI to answer questions.

政策法规.title: RegulatingAI: AI Regulations, Artificial Intelligence Regulation
政策法规.content: Navigating the Future of Global AI Regulations. Navigating the Future of Global AI Regulations. Regulating AI is dedicated to the ethos of responsible innovation. ### Regulating AI Podcast. Embark on a journey into the dynamic intersection of AI and regulation. Join our vibrant community to connect with experts, mentors, investors, and organizations to foster collaboration, innovation, and the responsible development of AI. Stay informed about the evolving landscape of AI regulation; receive the latest news from hearings in the U.S. Senate and Congress. ### US Senate Committee holds hearing on AI and the future of work. ### Senate Government Affairs Committee holds a hearing on AI acquisitions — 09/14/23. ### Senate Judiciary Subcommittee holds a hearing on AI legislation and oversight. Partner with our AI regulation and legislation website. Get latest updates, news, and information on all things AI legislations and policies! RegulatingAI, a non-profit organization, is dedicated to establishing a platform for grassroots advocacy concerning artificial intelligence (AI) regulation.

政策法规.content: The use of artificial intelligence in the EU is regulated by the AI Act, the world's first comprehensive AI law. Find out how it protects you.

政策法规.title: Executive Order Issued to Restrict State Regulation of AI
政策法规.content: *   [Administrative Law](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/administrative-law/). *   [Overview](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/administrative-law/). *   [Overview](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/corporate-and-business-law/). *   [Startups](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/corporate-and-business-law/startups/). *   [Tax](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/corporate-and-business-law/tax/). *   [Environmental Law](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/environmental-law/). *   [Overview](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/environmental-law/). *   [Regulatory](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/environmental-law/regulatory/). *   [Immigration Law](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/immigration-law/). *   [Overview](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/immigration-law/). *   [Visitors](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/immigration-law/visitors/). *   [Overview](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/international-business-law/). *   [Overview](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/labor-and-employment-law/). *   [Startups](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/corporate-and-business-law/startups). Executive Order 14365 was the administration’s seventh executive order supporting artificial intelligence (AI).[1](https://phillipslytle.com/executive-order-issued-to-restrict-state-regulation-of-artificial-intelligence/#footnote-1) This most recent executive order stipulates that a national AI policy is needed to enable the AI industry to innovate without being required to follow the AI laws of 50 different states. The administration asserted in the executive order that a national AI policy was needed to allow the AI industry to innovate without being required to follow a “[patchwork of 50 different regulatory regimes.](https://www.whitehouse.gov/presidential-actions/2025/12/eliminating-state-law-obstruction-of-national-artificial-intelligence-policy/)”[4](https://phillipslytle.com/executive-order-issued-to-restrict-state-regulation-of-artificial-intelligence/#footnote-4) However, the most recent Executive Order did not prohibit state AI laws; on the other hand, it allowed states to maintain their AI laws but if the AI laws were deemed onerous, the Administration would hold back congressionally authorized funding. *   [Administrative Law](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/administrative-law/). *   [Environmental Law](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/environmental-law/). *   [Immigration Law](https://phillipslytle.com/expertise/practices/immigration-law/).

行业趋势.title: AI trends of 2025: What's trending in AI in July? - Envato
行业趋势.content: Google’s Veo 3 makes this whole new take on fake news possible by adding native audio generation to AI video, creating anchors who don’t just look real — they sound real too. Filmmaker Travis Bible sparked the trend by creating warning videos for his parents about AI scams, featuring scenarios like reporters with sharks swimming behind them who suddenly announce “Just kidding, I’m not real.” The meta-awareness is the point — these videos teach media literacy by demonstrating how convincing fake news can look. The trend exploded after creators discovered they could use AI video generators like Google’s Veo 3 to create hyper-realistic historical characters doing the most mundane modern activities. * **Choose your tool:** Use AI image generators like our ImageGen or ChatGPT. For your AI video generation, try out Envato’s VideoGen. Start exploring the endless possibilities on Envato today, and join in on all the AI design trends in marketing your brand content.

行业趋势.title: The 9 AI Trends I'm Most Excited About Right Now | PCMag
行业趋势.content: # The 9 AI Trends I'm Most Excited About Right Now. After years of over-hyped AI news, some capable products are finally arriving from OpenAI, Microsoft, and others. I've been on staff at PCMag for three years after a career change from working in Big Tech on the West Coast for six years. The DeepSeek chatbot exploded out of China in late January, causing the US stock market to plunge and making American AI companies ask themselves, "How are they offering that ChatGPT-level of performance for a fraction of the cost?". The Trump administration is investigating what happened and trying to restrict the flow of chips to Chinese AI companies, *The New York Times*reports"). *Disclosure: Ziff Davis, PCMag's parent company, filed a lawsuit against OpenAI in April 2025, alleging it infringed Ziff Davis copyrights in training and operating its AI systems.*.
